title: Single- and/or double-membrane viral factories?
author: Romero-Brey I
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Once entering the cell host, viruses needs to interact with intracellular membranes in order to build up their replication organelles or factories, resulting in a remodeling of the cell membranes, the hallmark of virus infection. Positive (+) strand RNA viruses represent the largest group of RNA viruses. Among them two different architectures of replication factories (morphotypes) have been described so far, differing mainly in the amount of lipid bilayers they have: one or two
